info_enter_locator: Enter your locator and click Compute.
tip_save: Save callsign, locator, altitude and preferences.
tip_duration:
Total pass duration above the horizon
Green ≥ 5h, orange ≥ 2h, red < 2h
tip_el_max:
MAXIMUM ELEVATION

Maximum Moon altitude during this pass.
  Green ≥ 20° : low atmospheric loss
  Orange ≥ 10° : acceptable
  Red < 10° : high loss, high ground noise
tip_el_max_time: Time when the Moon reaches maximum elevation
tip_decl:
LUNAR DECLINATION

Angle of the Moon relative to the celestial equator.
Positive (+) = north, negative (−) = south.
Ranges from −28.6° to +28.6° over 18.6 years.
tip_distance:
EARTH-MOON DISTANCE (km)

Topocentric distance at maximum elevation.
Perigee: ~356,500 km, Apogee: ~406,700 km.
astronomy-engine calculations (~10m precision).
tip_extra_pl:
EXTRA PATH LOSS — Additional loss vs perigee

Extra PL = 40 × log₁₀(d / 356,500)
+0.0 dB = perigee (optimal), +5.8 dB = apogee.
tip_total_pl:
TOTAL PATH LOSS — Complete EME attenuation

Total = PL perigee + Extra PL
Change frequency to recalculate.
tip_moon_sun:
MOON-SUN ANGLE

180° = opposition (no solar noise)
0° = conjunction (max solar noise)
> 15° : no degradation
tip_libration:
LIBRATION RATE (deg/h)

< 0.10 : EXCELLENT
0.10-0.25 : GOOD
> 0.40 : POOR

Critical at 10 GHz and above.
tip_doppler:
DOPPLER SPREAD EME (Hz)

Frequency spread of the reflected signal
due to libration.
< 50 Hz : excellent, > 150 Hz : difficult.
tip_spread_min:
SPREAD MIN — Best moment of the pass

Minimum Doppler spread value over the entire
pass duration, with time ('magic window').
Sampled at 30 points.
tip_spread_max:
SPREAD MAX — Worst moment of the pass

Maximum Doppler spread value, with time.
Often at rise or set.
Sampled at 30 points.
tip_quality:
EME QUALITY INDEX (0 to 10)

Max elevation (30%/20%)
Duration (25%/10%)
Path loss (25%)
Moon-Sun (20%/15%)
Libration (0%/30% if ≥ 1 GHz)
tip_phase: Lunar phase (visual only, no EME effect)
tip_sked: Online EME QSO planning tool by SA5IKN
tip_local_time: Display in local time instead of UTC
tip_quality_filter: Filter by minimum EME quality (0 to 8)
tip_el_min:
MINIMUM ELEVATION — Usable horizon

Filters passes AND defines your MECHANICAL HORIZON.

If your dish is limited in low elevation,
set EL min to your threshold (e.g. 25°):

  → Only passes above 25° are shown
  → Rise/set times are computed at the moment
    the Moon crosses 25°
  → Displayed duration is your actual operation time
tip_row_click:
Click a pass row for detail every 30 minutes.
Click NOW for real-time dashboard.
msg_locator_missing: Enter your QRA locator (6 or 8 characters).
msg_locator_short: Locator must be at least 6 characters.
conv_intro: These conventions affect some values (distance, rise/set times). Defaults are optimized for EME (Earth-Moon-Earth radio amateurs). General astronomy software (mooncalc.org, Stellarium) often uses different conventions for visual observation.
conv_dist_explain: Topocentric: actual distance between your antenna and the Moon. Geocentric: from Earth's center (classical astronomical convention). Difference can reach ~6,378 km when Moon is at zenith.
conv_hor_explain: Geometric: Moon is risen when its center crosses the horizon (0°). Visual: upper limb + refraction (−0.83°). For EME, refraction is negligible for radio signals.
help_content:
How to use Moon Predictions

1. Station setup

Enter your callsign (optional), your QRA locator (6 or 8 chars) and your altitude. Click Save.

2. Computation

Click Compute to display moon passes over 30 days. Use 1-30 d / 31-60 d to navigate.

3. Filters

Adjust EL min and Score min. EL min = your mechanical horizon: if your dish can't go below 25°, set EL min to 25°. Times will be recomputed at the moment the Moon crosses your usable horizon.

4. Detail on click

Click a pass row for detail every 30 min. Click NOW for real-time dashboard.

5. Theme & Language

☀/☽ button to toggle light/dark. FR/NL/EN selector.
about_desc: Moon pass forecast for radio amateurs practicing EME (Earth-Moon-Earth). Web version.
